# Copilot Instructions — Toms Clippy Light

## What This Project Is

A native macOS 26 Tahoe menubar app (LSUIElement, no Dock icon) that maintains a clipboard history and lets the user paste any previous entry into the previously focused application. Written in Swift 6 / SwiftUI + AppKit. Unsigned for distribution (no Apple Developer ID); users remove the quarantine flag manually via `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine`.

Bundle ID: `com.tomschmidtdev.tomsclippylight`
Minimum OS: macOS 26.0 Tahoe
Swift: 6.0 (`SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY: complete`)
SPM dependency: [KeyboardShortcuts](https://github.com/sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts) ≥ 2.2.0

---

## Repository Layout

```
project.yml                    # XcodeGen spec — source of truth for project config
Makefile                       # make generate|build|test|install|zip|clean
TomsClippyLight/
  App.swift                    # @main entry, NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor
  AppDelegate.swift            # NSStatusItem lifecycle, panel management
  Models/
    ClipboardEntry.swift       # enum Kind { text, richText, image, files }
    Preferences.swift          # @Observable, backed by UserDefaults
  Core/
    ClipboardMonitor.swift     # Timer polling NSPasteboard.changeCount (0.4 s)
    HistoryStore.swift         # @Observable; dedup, truncate, pin, clear
    Paster.swift               # captureFocus / writeToPasteboard / paste
    PersistenceStore.swift     # JSON in ~/Library/Application Support/TomsClippyLight/
    LanguageBootstrap.swift    # sets AppleLanguages UserDefault before UI init
    KeyboardShortcutNames.swift
    SystemWrappers/
      PasteboardProtocol.swift
      WorkspaceProtocol.swift
      KeyboardSimulating.swift
      LaunchAtLoginService.swift
  UI/
    MenuBuilder.swift          # builds NSMenu on every right-click
    HistoryPopover.swift       # SwiftUI: search field + scrollable entry list
    HistoryRowView.swift       # single row (text preview / image thumbnail / file icon)
    SettingsView.swift         # TabView shell
    GeneralSettingsTab.swift
    ShortcutSettingsTab.swift
    AboutTab.swift
  Localization/
    Localizable.xcstrings      # String Catalog, EN (base) + DE
    L10n.swift                 # generated accessor enum
TomsClippyLightTests/          # Swift Testing unit tests
  Fakes/                       # FakePasteboard, FakeWorkspace, etc.
TomsClippyLightUITests/        # XCUITest for Settings window
.github/workflows/
  build.yml                    # CI: build + unit tests on every PR / push to main
  release.yml                  # CD: tag v* → GitHub Release with .zip + SHA-256
```

**Never edit `TomsClippyLight.xcodeproj` directly.** It is generated by XcodeGen from `project.yml`. Run `xcodegen generate` (or `make generate`) after any change to `project.yml`.

---

## Build & Workflow

### Prerequisites

- macOS 26 Tahoe
- Xcode 26+
- XcodeGen: `brew install xcodegen`

### Makefile targets

| Target | What it does |
|---|---|
| `make generate` | `xcodegen generate` — regenerate `.xcodeproj` |
| `make build` | Debug build (generates project first) |
| `make test` | Unit + UI tests |
| `make archive` | Release archive (signed with Apple Development cert if found, otherwise ad-hoc) |
| `make install` | `archive` then copies `.app` to `/Applications` |
| `make zip` | `archive` then packages distributable `.zip` into `build/` |
| `make release-local` | Same as `zip`, prints artifact path |
| `make clean` | Remove `build/`, `DerivedData/`, generated `.xcodeproj` |

### Local signing

The Makefile auto-detects an Apple Development certificate from the keychain via `security find-identity -v -p codesigning`. If found, it signs with that cert (required for Accessibility TCC permissions to persist across rebuilds). If not found, it falls back to ad-hoc (`-`).

**Important:** Ad-hoc signing changes the binary hash on every build. macOS TCC tracks apps by code signature. If you use ad-hoc signing, you must re-grant Accessibility permission after every rebuild.

### CI (GitHub Actions)

CI always uses ad-hoc signing and passes:
```
CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="-" CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=YES DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=""
```

### Version bump rule

**Every build that gets installed or released must increment `CFBundleVersion` in `project.yml`.** `CFBundleShortVersionString` only changes for user-visible releases.

---

## Architecture

### Data Flow

```
NSPasteboard ──(0.4 s poll)──▶ ClipboardMonitor ──▶ HistoryStore
                                                          │
                                   ┌──────────────────────┤
                                   ▼                      ▼
                           MenuBuilder               HistoryPopover
                           (right-click)      (hotkey ⇧⌘V / left-click)
                                   │                      │
                                   └──────────┬───────────┘
                                              ▼
                                           Paster ──▶ previously focused app
```

### Key Components

**ClipboardMonitor** — Polls `NSPasteboard.changeCount` every 0.4 s. Skips entries with `NSPasteboardTypeConcealed` (password manager entries). Constructs a `ClipboardEntry` and calls `HistoryStore.add(_:)`.

**HistoryStore** — `@Observable`. Maintains `pinned: [ClipboardEntry]` and `recent: [ClipboardEntry]`. Deduplicates by content hash (removes older duplicate, inserts new at front). Truncates `recent` to `historySize`. Pinned entries survive truncation.

**Paster** — Three-phase paste:
1. `captureFocus()` — called before the history panel opens; records frontmost app bundle ID and PID.
2. `writeToPasteboard(_:)` — writes the chosen entry to `NSPasteboard.general`.
3. `paste(_:)` — writes, reactivates previous app (`app.activate()`), polls until app is frontmost (up to 400 ms), then posts a CGEvent CMD+V via `.cgSessionEventTap`. Requires Accessibility permission.

**AppDelegate** — Manages `NSStatusItem`, the `KeyablePanel`, and keyboard event monitors. Left-click opens the history panel; right-click builds and shows the `NSMenu`.

**MenuBuilder** — Rebuilds the `NSMenu` from scratch on every right-click so entries are always current.

---

## Critical AppKit / macOS Gotchas

These are non-obvious constraints that have caused bugs. Read before editing panel or paste code.

### 1. NSPanel must override `canBecomeKey`

`NSPanel` with `.borderless` styleMask returns `false` from `canBecomeKey` by default. This prevents **any text field inside the panel from receiving keyboard input**. The project uses a private subclass:

```swift
private final class KeyablePanel: NSPanel {
    override var canBecomeKey: Bool { true }
    override var canBecomeMain: Bool { false }
}
```

Do not replace `KeyablePanel` with plain `NSPanel`.

### 2. Use `activate()`, not `activate(from:)`

`NSRunningApplication.activate(from:)` requires the calling application to currently be frontmost. This app is an `LSUIElement` (background-only) app — it loses frontmost status immediately when `closeHistoryPanel()` is called and `historyPanel?.orderOut(nil)` executes. By that point, `activate(from:)` silently fails.

`activate()` (deprecated macOS 14, but still functional) has no such requirement. It is used intentionally.

### 3. Capture focus BEFORE activating our panel

`paster.captureFocus()` must be called before `NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)`. Once our panel becomes key, the frontmost app is us — not the user's previous app.

### 4. Arrow-key navigation uses a local NSEvent monitor

SwiftUI focus state is unreliable for up/down arrow navigation in an `NSPanel`. `AppDelegate` installs a local `NSEvent` monitor that intercepts ↑ / ↓ / Return / Escape key events and posts `NotificationCenter` messages (`historyMoveUp`, `historyMoveDown`, `historySelectCurrent`). `HistoryPopover` reacts to those notifications via `onReceive`. The monitor is removed when the panel closes.

### 5. TCC and code signing

macOS TCC tracks Accessibility permission grants by code signature. Ad-hoc signing (CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="-") produces a different hash every build, orphaning TCC grants. For local development where auto-paste must work, sign with an Apple Development certificate (the Makefile does this automatically).

### 6. `firstTextField(in:)` must filter for editable fields

The recursive NSTextField search skips hidden and non-editable text fields:
```swift
if let tf = view as? NSTextField, !tf.isHidden, tf.isEditable { return tf }
```
SwiftUI renders label text as non-editable `NSTextField` subviews. Without the `isEditable` check, the wrong view would receive first-responder status.

---

## Testing

### Philosophy

- **Classicist (state-based)** for pure-logic components: `HistoryStore`, `ClipboardEntry`, `Preferences`, `PersistenceStore`.
- **London / Mockist** for components that call system APIs: `ClipboardMonitor`, `Paster`. These use protocol fakes injected via initialiser.
- **XCUITest** for the Settings window (smoke test only — tab switching, stepper bounds).

### Protocol Fakes (in `TomsClippyLightTests/Fakes/`)

| Fake | Replaces |
|---|---|
| `FakePasteboard` | `PasteboardProtocol` / `NSPasteboard` |
| `FakeWorkspace` | `WorkspaceProtocol` / `NSWorkspace` |
| `FakeKeyboardSimulator` | `KeyboardSimulating` / CGEvent posting |
| `FakeLaunchAtLoginService` | `LaunchAtLoginService` / `SMAppService` |

Inject fakes via initialisers — never use `NSPasteboard.general` or `NSWorkspace.shared` in tests.

### Running tests

```bash
make test
```

---

## Localisation

String Catalog at `TomsClippyLight/Localization/Localizable.xcstrings`. EN is the base language. DE is the full translation. Access strings only via the `L10n` enum in `L10n.swift` — never use raw string literals for UI text.

Language can be overridden in Settings. `LanguageBootstrap.applyIfNeeded(_:)` sets `AppleLanguages` in UserDefaults before the UI is initialised, then terminates and relaunches the app for the language change to take effect.

---

## Release Process

1. Bump `CFBundleShortVersionString` and `CFBundleVersion` in `project.yml`.
2. Commit and push.
3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z && git push origin vX.Y.Z`
4. GitHub Actions (`release.yml`) archives (unsigned), zips, computes SHA-256, creates GitHub Release.

---

## What This App Does NOT Do

- No network access.
- No iCloud sync.
- No Sandboxing (Hardened Runtime disabled — required for CGEvent posting).
- No notarisation (unsigned binary).
- Not available on the App Store.
